meta.title: SQL Pro — Open-Source SQLite Database Manager
meta.description: Free, open-source SQLite database manager with SQLCipher encryption, visual diff preview, Monaco SQL editor, and AI-powered query tools. For macOS, Windows, and Linux.

hero.title: The Modern, Open-Source SQLite Database Manager
hero.subtitle: Professional SQLite Database Manager
hero.description: Query, edit, and visualize your SQLite databases with a powerful Monaco editor, SQLCipher encryption, visual diff preview, and AI-powered tools — all in a free, cross-platform desktop app.

features.title: Powerful Features,
features.subtitle: From quick queries to complex schema analysis — a complete toolkit designed for developers who work with SQLite every day.

database.title: Multi-Database Support
database.description: Open multiple SQLite and SQLCipher databases simultaneously with tabbed connections. Manage all your databases from a single, unified interface.

query.title: Monaco SQL Editor
query.description: Write SQL with full IntelliSense autocomplete, syntax highlighting, Vim mode, and query history. The same editor that powers VS Code.

data.title: Visual Data Editing
data.description: Edit data inline with spreadsheet-like controls, preview every change with a visual diff before committing, and run bulk operations with confidence.

compare.title: Schema & Data Compare
compare.description: Compare schemas and data between connections

visualization.title: ER Diagram & Schema Visualization
visualization.description: Explore your database structure with interactive ER diagrams, browse tables, views, indexes, and trace foreign key relationships at a glance.

security.title: SQLCipher Encryption
security.description: Open and manage encrypted SQLite databases with full SQLCipher support. Encryption keys are stored securely in your system's native keychain.

crossPlatform.title: macOS, Windows & Linux
crossPlatform.description: One app, three platforms. Native Electron builds for macOS (Universal), Windows (x64), and Linux (AppImage & deb).

screenshots.title: See It in Action
screenshots.subtitle: A modern interface designed for speed and clarity

screenshots.table: High-performance data grid with inline editing

testimonials.label: Testimonials
testimonials.title: Loved by
testimonials.subtitle: Hear from developers who use SQL Pro to manage their SQLite databases every day.

quotes.developer: SQL Pro completely changed how I work with SQLite. The Monaco editor with IntelliSense is a game changer — it feels like writing code in VS Code.
quotes.datascientist: The visual diff preview alone is worth it. I can review data changes before committing, which saves me hours every week and prevents mistakes.
quotes.indie: As an indie developer, I need tools that are fast, reliable, and don't get in the way. SQL Pro nails all three — and it's open source.

opensource.label: Open Source
opensource.title: Built in the Open,
opensource.description: SQL Pro is fully open source under the MIT license. Audit the code, contribute features, report issues, or fork it for your own needs. No vendor lock-in, ever.

faq.label: FAQ
faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about SQL Pro.

free.question: Is SQL Pro free to use?
free.answer: Yes! The core edition of SQL Pro is open source and free under the MIT license. You can download and use it for personal and commercial projects at no cost. Pro features like SQLCipher encryption and AI tools are available through a paid subscription.

platforms.question: Which platforms does SQL Pro support?
platforms.answer: SQL Pro runs natively on macOS (Universal binary for Intel and Apple Silicon), Windows (x64), and Linux (x64 via AppImage and deb packages). All platforms receive the same features and updates.

sqlite.question: What SQLite features are supported?
sqlite.answer: SQL Pro supports all standard SQLite features including views, triggers, indexes, foreign keys, virtual tables, and full-text search (FTS5). It also supports WAL mode and concurrent connections for high-performance workflows.

encryption.question: How does SQLCipher encryption work?
encryption.answer: SQL Pro integrates SQLCipher to open and manage encrypted SQLite databases. Your encryption keys are stored securely in your system's native keychain — macOS Keychain, Windows Credential Manager, or Linux Secret Service — so they never touch disk in plain text.

updates.question: How do I get updates?
updates.answer: SQL Pro checks for updates automatically. When a new version is available, you'll be notified and can update with one click. You can also download releases manually from GitHub.

support.question: Where can I get help?
support.answer: Community support is available through GitHub Discussions. For bug reports, use GitHub Issues. Pro subscribers also receive priority email support and faster response times.
